description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Observed on Atlas (first implementation-tier adopter, PR #60): implementation closure hashes shift across compile contexts even with merge-on-flush (ia9) in place and per-context determinism holding (consecutive spec.check runs are byte-identical).

Evidence: (1) a dep bump touching ONLY spec_led_ex's own file_diff.ex shifted implementation hashes for ~53 of 101 Atlas subjects (forced clean recompile + fresh manifest, hashers unchanged); (2) CI runs (Linux, shared-workflow compile) consistently report large drift lists vs locally-seeded baselines (macOS); (3) after a from-scratch reseed the working baseline differed from the prior seed by only 8 entries while ~50 drift warnings persisted vs the committed copy — the drift detector and the seeded file disagree about what changed.

Consequence: branch_guard_realization_drift on the implementation tier cannot graduate warning->error for any consumer until hash inputs are normalized to be compile-context-invariant (or the baseline becomes CI-seeded by convention). Suggest auditing hash inputs for beam-metadata/path/dep-version leakage and adding a cross-compile determinism test (compile --force twice; compile on two MIX_ENVs) to the ia9 determinism contract.

comments.text: Evidence corpus frozen at ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/corpora/atlas-l05-snapshot-2026-07-23 (ticket specled_-6pq). Atlas a1e9c670 @ spec_led_ex ca9cec8. KEY FINDING: macOS and CI drift sets are IDENTICAL (130 findings: 53 api_boundary + 77 implementation) at same SHA + same toolchain (Elixir 1.19.5/OTP 28 both sides) — standing drift is stale-baseline, NOT env-sensitivity; 668/798 entries provably hash identically cross-env. Env-sensitivity evidence is historical (dep-bump flip 433a366d, pre-vmi-reseed 46a4b2af); attribution study must test whether those mechanisms still reproduce on current specled main (ab5beb6/1a837d3/c228bff landed since). Full macOS recomputed world captured; CI recomputed values are the one known gap (needs artifact-upload tweak if required). Atlas is now UNFROZEN to bump ≥0.4.x — one-time o40 api_boundary drift expected (no hasher_version bump), absorb in dedicated dep-bump PR.

title: implementation-tier hashes are compile-context-sensitive (drift noise across recompiles/environments)
